data.narrative:
Xage Security is a Series B defense and security company that has raised $80M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers in the Defense & Security sector at similar stages. The peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $80M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $152M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $1.2B median valuation, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The five named peers (Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, and Castelion and Defense Unicorns each at $1.0B) anchor this range.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $129M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median implied value of $676M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression methods yield significantly lower valuations than peer comparables, suggesting either that Xage's profile diverges meaningfully from peer benchmarks or that early-stage capital raised is not predictive of later-stage valuations in this sector. Resolution of this gap requires deeper diligence into revenue traction, customer concentration, and relative technology positioning versus named peers.
